title: aphanochilus communis
intro: Aphanochilus communis, also known as Kevra or Kevra flower, is a plant species commonly found in tropical regions, particularly in India and Sri Lanka. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. Aphanochilus communis is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of oil, powder, or decoction, and is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments.
